machinery_genes.quote: EscI self-associates and interacts with the outer membrane secretin EscC
machinery_genes.note: Sal-Man 2012 (EPEC) names EscC as the outer-membrane secretin of the LEE T3SS. Same gene present and named 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 EDL933 LEE Table 2 (Perna 1998, 10.1128/iai.66.8.3810-3817.1998); EDL933 and Sakai share the LEE-encoded Esc-Esp injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escJ listed in Perna 1998 Table 2 with proposed function 'Type III secretion apparatus' in the O157:H7 EDL933 LEE. SctJ inner-ring role from apparatus brief vocabulary.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escD annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). SctD inner-membrane ring role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escR annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). SctR export-apparatus role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escS annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). SctS export-apparatus role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escT annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). SctT export-apparatus role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: EscI interacts with EscU, the integral membrane protein that is linked to substrate specificity switching
machinery_genes.note: Sal-Man 2012 (EPEC) names EscU as the integral-membrane substrate-switching component. escU also annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE).

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escV annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE), 675 aa. SctV major export-apparatus ring role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL, of the export apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo 2014 (EPEC) names EscN as the export-apparatus ATPase; independently called 'the type III ATPase EscN' (Aguilera 2012, 10.1016/j.biocel.2012.03.002, PMID 22433988) and 'the T3SS membrane-associated ATPase EscN' (Thomas 2005, 10.1111/j.1365-2958.2005.04802.x, PMID 16135239). escN also in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE).

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stalk / FliJ analog (positive regulator of EscN)
machinery_genes.quote: EscO, a functional and structural analog of the flagellar FliJ protein, is a positive regulator of EscN ATPase activity of the enteropathogenic Escherichia coli injectisome
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo 2014 (EPEC; previously Orf15/EscA). FliJ/YscO/SctO-family central stalk of the export apparatus. Strain transfer from EPEC to the homologous Sakai LEE injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL, of the export apparatus
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo 2014 (EPEC) names EscL as the negative regulator of EscN within the export apparatus; SctL/YscL/MxiN-family ATPase stator. Strain transfer from EPEC to the homologous Sakai LEE injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: rOrf8/EscI, a poorly characterized locus of enterocyte effacement-encoded protein, functions as the inner rod protein of the T3SS of EPEC
machinery_genes.note: Sal-Man 2012 establishes EscI as the YscI/PrgJ/MxiI (SctI) inner-rod homolog of the attaching-and-effacing T3SS. Strain transfer from EPEC to the homologous Sakai LEE injectisome.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: escF annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE), 73 aa (small needle-subunit size). SctF needle-filament role from apparatus brief; EscF is the established EPEC/EHEC needle protein.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: sepQ (= escQ) annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). SctQ cytoplasmic C-ring/sorting-platform role from apparatus brief.

machinery_genes.quote: The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: sepZ annotated 'Type III secretion apparatus' in Perna 1998 Table 2 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). Listed as apparatus (sep gene), not an effector. Family mapping to SctZ is approximate; role kept generic to avoid over-claiming.

machinery_genes.quote: EspL2 is encoded by the phage-like element SpLE3, is secreted by the sepL mutant and is translocated through the Esc-Esp T3SS
machinery_genes.note: Tobe 2006 (Sakai) names SepL in the context of the Esc-Esp T3SS that translocates the SpLE3 effectors; SepL is the LEE-encoded gatekeeper (SctW/InvE/MxiC analog) controlling the translocator-to-effector secretion switch. Directly ties the SpLE3 effectors of this instance to the LEE apparatus.

machinery_genes.role: secretion apparatus / gatekeeper partner of SepL
machinery_genes.quote: Secretion of GAPDH is increased in DeltasepD mutants and abolished in mutants defective in the type III ATPase EscN
machinery_genes.note: Aguilera 2012 (EPEC) treats SepD as a component governing type III secretion (SepD/SepL gatekeeper complex), alongside the EscN ATPase. sepD also a 'sep' apparatus gene in Perna 1998 (O157:H7 EDL933 LEE). Lower-confidence family mapping; included as documented secretion-apparatus component, not an effector or chaperone.

notes: SpLE3 (Sakai prophage-like element 3) carries T3SS EFFECTORS, not an apparatus. The effectors of this instance (ECs3485, ECs3855=EspL2, ECs3857=NleB1, ECs3858=NleE) are translocated through the LEE-encoded Esc-Esp injectisome, a SEPARATE chromosomal locus, not by genes within SpLE3 itself. This is the classic separated-paper case: Tobe et al. 2006 (PNAS, Sakai strain) states 'EspL2 is encoded by the phage-like element SpLE3 ... and is translocated through the Esc-Esp T3SS' and that the LEE 'encodes the Esc-Esp type III secretion system'. The machinery answer key is therefore the LEE injectisome of O157:H7. Per-gene naming for the O157:H7 apparatus comes from Perna et al. 1998 (Infect Immun), the complete LEE sequence of EHEC O157:H7 EDL933, whose Table 2 annotates escC, escD, escF, escJ, escN, escR, escS, escT, escU, escV, sepQ and sepZ as 'Type III secretion apparatus' and whose body text states 'The esc (20) and sep (39) genes encode components of a type III secretory apparatus.' EDL933 and Sakai are both O157:H7 and share the LEE-encoded Esc-Esp T3SS, so this is a within-serotype transfer (EDL933 -> Sakai). Functional roles for EscN (ATPase), EscO (FliJ-analog stalk), EscL (ATPase negative regulator/stator), EscI (inner rod), EscC (outer-membrane secretin), EscU (switch) and SepD/SepL (gatekeeper complex) are taken from EPEC mechanistic studies of the homologous LEE T3SS (Romo-Castillo 2014, Sal-Man 2012, Aguilera 2012, Thomas 2005); EPEC->O157 transfer noted per gene. Deliberately EXCLUDED: the SpLE3 effectors themselves (EspL2/NleB/NleE etc.), the translocon proteins EspA/EspB/EspD and their chaperones (CesD etc.), effector chaperones (CesT, CesAB), and the LEE transcriptional regulators (Ler/GrlA/GrlR), none of which are injectisome apparatus per the type brief. Lowest-confidence calls: SepZ and SepD family-to-Sct mapping is approximate (kept role generic); they are included because Perna 1998 annotates them as apparatus 'sep' genes of the O157 LEE. EscF needle and EscI rod assignments rest on the EPEC functional papers plus the Perna O157 gene list.
